statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, count the number of bits that need to be flipped to make all numbers in the array equal to zero. A bit is considered flipped if it is 1 and needs to change to 0, or vice versa. You may assume that all numbers in the array are represented in binary.
statement.inputFormat: Array of integers, where each integer is represented as a binary number.
statement.outputFormat: The count of bits that need to be flipped to make all numbers in the array equal to zero.
statement.notes: You may use bitwise operations to solve this problem.

hints.text: Calculate the total number of flips for each number, then sum them up.

editorial.title: Flipping Bits to Zero Count
editorial.intuition: You can use a bitwise operation to find the number of flips for each number, then sum them up.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to calculate the number of flips for each number in the array, then sum them up.

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
